name: Zhu Bajie
description: Zhu Bajie, also known as Pigsy or Zhu Wuneng, is one of the four main characters in the classic Chinese novel *Journey to the West*. Originally a celestial marshal named Tianpeng Yuan Shuai, he was banished to the mortal realm after drunkenly flirting with the moon goddess Chang'e. Reborn as a pig-monster, he terrorized the countryside until Guanyin, the bodhisattva of compassion, recruited him to join Tang Sanzang's pilgrimage to retrieve Buddhist scriptures from the West. Zhu Bajie is characterized by his insatiable appetite, laziness, and lustful desires. Despite his flaws, he possesses considerable strength and skill with his nine-toothed rake, and ultimately plays a vital role in protecting Tang Sanzang from demons and other dangers. He represents the struggle with earthly desires and the potential for redemption through devotion and perseverance. Though often portrayed as comedic relief, his journey reflects the human condition and the challenges of overcoming personal weaknesses to achieve enlightenment. He is a complex character whose faults and virtues make him relatable and enduringly popular in Chinese culture and beyond. His transformation from a celestial being to a flawed pig demon and ultimately to a purified disciple symbolizes the cyclical nature of karma and the possibility of spiritual growth. He embodies the common human struggle against temptation and the possibility of achieving enlightenment through perseverance and self-discipline.
subtitle: The Pigsy Pilgrim of Journey to the West
achievements: Joins the Journey to the West

keyMyths.title: Flirting with Chang'e
keyMyths.description: Zhu Bajie, as Tianpeng Yuan Shuai, was banished from heaven for drunkenly flirting with the moon goddess Chang'e. This incident led to his reincarnation as a pig monster.

keyMyths.title: Joining the Pilgrimage
keyMyths.description: Guanyin convinced Zhu Bajie to join Tang Sanzang's pilgrimage, promising him redemption for his past sins. He became a disciple and protector of the monk.

keyMyths.title: Fighting Demons
keyMyths.description: Throughout the journey, Zhu Bajie used his strength and rake to battle various demons and monsters. While often lazy, he contributed significantly to the group's safety.

sources.author: Wu Cheng'en
sources.description: The classic Chinese novel detailing the pilgrimage of Tang Sanzang and his disciples.
sources.text: The classic Chinese novel detailing the pilgrimage of Tang Sanzang and his disciples.
